violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Pizza was observed held at 121-130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Pizza was reheated at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Ranch dressing was observed held above fill line at prep unit #1 at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Reviewed cold holding procedures, dressing was moved into bottom of prep unit at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Rice and pasta salad was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

(Rust cleaner) was stored with canned tomato sauce. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Working containers of sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Lowboy door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026
The wall in the wareashing area has an accumulation of black residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Pizza was observed held at 111-115 degrees Fahrenheit in the pizza hot hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, pizza had been in the hot hold unit for a 1/2 hour. Food was reheated to 175 & 185 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Hot hold unit was taken out of service at time of inspection. Establishment must not use the pizza hot hold unit unless it can maintain an internal food temperature of 135 degrees Fahrenheit or higher. Per person in charge, pizza will be held on top of the oven. Pizza temperature must be monitored. If pizza is not maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above on top of the oven, establishment must discontinue using the top of the oven to hot hold. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

Pizza was observed held at 107 degrees Fahrenheit in the pizza hot hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, pizza had been in the unit for about an hour. Pizza was transferred to the walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. The ambient air temperature was observed held at 105 degrees Fahrenheit. Pizza hot hold unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

The sink used for food preparation and ice machine are lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety certification course during time of inspection. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Menu is lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. Per person in charge, establishment is in the process of printing new menus. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per person in charge, an application was submitted yesterday but certificate has not been processed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Per person in charge, they are in the process of printing new menus. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
Food was observed stored uncovered in the prep units and walk-in cooler. Food must be covered to prevent contamination. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
Menu is lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

An employee was observed filling up a container at the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

Fish was observed thawing on the floor. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
Food was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
Food was observed stored uncovered in the prep units and walk-in cooler. Food must be covered to prevent contamination during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

A knife was observed stored on an unclean surface (under a container) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the prep area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
